title: [Idea] Temporal Replay conference review
reference_observations: The stronger reference posts worked because they were concrete, personal, and anchored in real moments: a build, a launch, a talk, a room full of engineers. But they mostly stayed at the level of announcement, event recap, or broad excitement. What they missed was the specific human texture in this Raw Idea: teaching as a TA, the Nexus workshop with Mason, Melissa’s AI session, the surprise Tiki room, live music with game visuals, glow-in-the-dark cotton candy, and the bigger systems takeaway that durable execution is becoming core AI infrastructure. This post can differentiate by being more grounded in what was actually seen and felt while still extracting one sharp lesson for engineers building AI in production.
